note: SOP CONTENT manifest (Corpus 5 / P4-03). This pins the ONE canonical SOP V2 library asset that 32-command-center-setup/scripts/ingest-sop-library.sh downloads, and the row population a fully-ingested box must reach.
count_derivation: The asset holds 2618 JSONL records / 2617 distinct slugs. ingest-sop-library.py keys every row on `sop_ + sha256(slug)` (a collision-resistant deterministic PRIMARY KEY), so all 2617 distinct slugs land as exactly 2617 distinct `sops` rows. 2617 is therefore the DERIVED canonical population, not a guess -- and it reconciles with the field: a fully-populated box reads 2640 = 2617 library + 23 CC autoSeedStarterSOPs starters (the two id-spaces are disjoint, verified 0 collisions). U119 (same as U078): the old 60-char truncation caused 62 long slugs to collapse (2555 rows); the collision-resistant hash restores all 2617.
embeddings: SEPARATE CONCERN -- this manifest covers SOP CONTENT rows ONLY. Ingesting these rows performs ZERO embedding API calls and therefore costs the client NOTHING. The embedding vectors for this library are governed by shared-utils/sop-embed-once/SOP-EMBEDDINGS-MANIFEST.json, which as of this writing carries asset_rebuild_required:true (no embeddings asset has ever been published). Until that asset exists, newly-ingested rows are UNEMBEDDED, and embedding them is an explicit, operator-initiated action on the CLIENT's own Gemini key -- never a silent side effect of an update.
consumed_by: 32-command-center-setup/scripts/ingest-sop-library.sh (tag + sha256 hard gate + already-populated skip gate)
update-skills.sh Step U6c (update-path ingestion + fail-loud gate)
